title: One fabulous piece of comfort and engineering
text: The headpiece just arrived and it is truly superb. We purchased this headset to watch and listen to music lessons on my computer while we practiced on my keyboard. It was a nuisance switching separate headsets. This truly gorgeous headset is fantastic. It feeds the sound through each ear piece. I am amazed at the sound reproduction and very pleased with the comfortable ear cushions and especially how they fit over my ears (with hearing aids). It has two plug apertures ( one on each headpiece, the 6.3mm plug fits my Casio keyboard, the smaller plug 3.5mm,  in the PC. Actually with the plug adapters available today it really wouldn't make much difference what orientation they installed in the ear piece. The wire & plug set included worked out perfectly. Unfortunately, we bought another set of wired plugs also to make sure of the orientation as we couldn't wait for it to get here and not be able to use it immediately. It performs beautifully.  PS. a note of thanks to the manufacturer repr

title: Updated Review:  Seller/Manufacturer reached out about problem headphones
text:
Final Review Update, April 2020

The OneOdio CS team emailed me to request I increase my review star rating. To be fair, they'd sent me a new set of headphones to try out. But it's not even ONE YEAR since my purchase of the original headphones, and now TWO OneOdio headphones have failed. The replacement (thanks, OneOdio) crackles badly when using the line with the microphone, making videoconferencing impractical. And without even touching the mic button, my Itunes folder is constantly randomly opened--in the middle of conference calls for work. Extremely unprofessional.

I don't know what has happened to the headphone industry. We used to go through one headset every three to five years. Then a few years ago, while taking better care of our headsets--including wrapping the cord gently according to professional electronics instructional videos, gently placing our headsets in padded protective cases...--our headsets stopped lasting even a single year. Two OneOdios in

title: Great headphones... Great price
text: Great cans for the price... I work in radio and have found when I don't spend $100 on headphones they last longer but I still want good quality... These come with a convenient carrying bag and two cords... One regular 3.5 mm cord that you can plug into your phone, or a 6.35 jack for studio/dj use. Soft ear cushions and the noise canceling is great!

title: Not worth it
text:
Received today, tried them on and ...
They're huge, bulky and heavy to start with, so much that my head was actually hurting on top from the weight.
I turned them on, connected to my phone and tried them out; the "noise canceling" does not work whatsoever, I heard everything that was said to me by others and all other random noise, including my washer and dryer in my basement.
I do not know about how battery life is, but given my experience with them otherwise, I am not keeping them long enough to find out.
Picture included to show actual size, which is not the same as advertised.
The ear padding is comfortable enough and it did connect rather quickly to my device.

title: Great for a month
text:
UPDATE [9/21/2017]: After being contacted from customer support on this issue, I'm updating these to 4 stars. Customer support was very kind and helpful with this situation and issued me a replacement. Audio quality is spot on. I use these with 25% volume on my computer and adjust the media in the actual program and, in all instances, it was never blasting my eardrums or making me strain to hear the audio. It's crisp and clear and, especially for a work environment, blocks out a good amount of minor chatter and other noises. As long as you don't have your music blasting, you can still hear if someone is trying to get your attention.

I've had these for about a month now and I use them for work, so there is some pretty heavy usage 5 days of the week. While they were great while they lasted, the left cup is spotty now. In order to get sound out of it, I have to have the audio jack partially in, rather than completely in. The construction is great, though

title: Incredible headphones with only one issue
text:
I love these headphones. They are amazingly clear and the battery is no joke. I charged them when I first got them and haven't again over a month later. They're still at 70%!

Only issue I have is with the 3.5mm cable that comes with them. First, it's way too short. Fine for use with your phone or something, but too short for plugging into a desktop PC set on the floor when working at a desk, or with my guitar amp. Second, I think the notch on the one end is a bit of an annoyance because I can't just use any audio jack cable if I want to replace it. Only one with that notch with fit into the headphone.

Mic isn't great either, but that's secondary for me. Not that big of a deal.

**UPDATE** I've had to recharge them now for the first time on Feb.6, 2023. Incredible battery.**

title: Poorly made; no noise canceling; awkward design
text:
I suspect this might be a vendor that pays customers to give good reviews because reviews did not at all reflect the quality of this product. Not well-made, no noise canceling quality whatsoever, and, as you can see from the photos, the cheap plastic easily broke. I had the product for six weeks and did not use it excessively to warrant this kind of damage. It is past the window to return, which is disappointing, but I’m still going to check if there’s some sort of warranty.
Also the way it is designed is awkward. It’s not intuitive as far as which way the earpieces should turn. It’s just as easy to put them on the wrong way as it is to put them on the correct way, which is annoying because you often have to fix them again. They twist in a really awkward way. //// Update: Emailed vendor a week ago. Still haven’t heard back

title: My wife loves these for her electric piano
text:
These OneOdio Wired Over Ear Headphones Studio Monitor & Mixing DJ Stereo Headsets are excellent and my wife and I are both thrilled she has them!

My wife had been a pianist since junior high, but in recent years, been unable to play as we've moved enough, hauling a piano around was out of the question. I was recently able to get her an electric paint (here on Amazon) and have been supporting her playing. However, two people + small home = less practice, so she immediately began researching headphones. She found these and based on other's reviews, decided they were the ones she wanted - and is thrilled she picked them! Along with fitting perfectly and feeling good while wearing, she finds the sound outstanding with her piano, and I can work, watch tv, listen to music, do whatever, and we do not bother one another. In other words, I cannot hear her, she cannot hear my stuff, and we are able to do what we need to in our small space.

They have great padding for the e

title: For the price, I can solidly recommend these to anyone!
text:
UPDATE (3/29/2019):
-----
These headphones are still going strong for me. For the price, I can solidly recommend these to anyone!

UPDATE (12/7/2018):
-----
It's been a few months and I haven't had a single problem with these headphones! They sound good and the modular cord is awesome! Also, the cords are easy to coil and don't get tangled like the thinner ones that come on some headphones.

ORIGINAL REVIEW
-----
So far these headphones have performed pretty well. By no means am I an audiophile so that may influence my opinion a bit.

The overall construction is about what you would expect for the price: they feel very plastic-y and like you could break them without much effort. That said, they don't feel so cheap that you expect them to break the first time you unfold them. You can tell most of the cost went into the cable and speakers.

The cables feel solid and are quite flexible. It's a bit of a shame that the bl

title: These came with a shorter cord than it claims
text: It says a 9'8" cord for easy movement around dj booth or laying in bed. I was pretty upset to find out my cord was about 4' I called customer service and got the run around. Haven't been able to listen with them yet. I'm away for Christmas. I will give 4 stars if the sound is great. Not HAPPY WITH CUSTOMER SERVICE!

title: They sound GREAT but don't last.
text:
Been a DJ for over 35 years, and I've gone through more than my share of headphones. Expensive ones, cheap ones, and ones that cost way too much.

The truth is, most "premium" headphones these days use pretty much the same components. Heavier magnets make for bigger better drivers, and thicker gauge wires, along with some smooth circuitry... Well, you can easily spend $500 or $50 and if you research hard enough, you'll find there's not much difference in sound quality - all things being equal.

I have tried 3 sets of these headphones, in their different levels of padding, etc. And first lets get this out of the way:

They sound GREAT
These are LOUD
They will deliver the BASS - which is impressive despite not requiring phantom power (like Beats, etc.)

The problem is their overall build quality. If you are a casual user, not caring about brand names, by all means, run, don't walk - to throw these in your cart. For less than $40 you'd be

title: A disappointment
text:
9/25/19. UPDATE:
OneOdio sent me a new set of phones. Wow...what a difference a properly functioning pair makes!
There is still the issue of the orientation of the earcups, THat's in the design. I can live with that. But the big difference is in the overall quality of the headphones.
The replacement set has a different cord. The previous one had a woven covering. They look like they would last forever. The new set has a vinyl insulating covering. I suspect this is to more easily accommodate the new built-in microphone. Yes, they have added a mic into the cord of the phones. A nice feature.
The very good news, though, is this set of phones has two working channels, so listening to stereo music is now possible. The sound quality is good. I would like a bit more bass response, but I really cannot complain about the sound, given the price of these phones. And with sound coming through both channels, I have no problem listening to music for an hour or more.
The feat

title: No Clairity
text: All base no highs, has a muffled sound. I would highly suggest going with a name brand over these.

title: Can’t get any better
text: I use this everyday.  All day.  Battery life is outstanding.  Quality of audio is great, too!  I have bought this brand many times.  I have bought Bluetooth and wired and have not been disappointed yet.  You cannot beat this price.  I have recommended this headset to many friends and family while continuing to share my experience with this product.  I am very happy and will continue to be a customer for the long haul.  Completely affordable and fits comfortably on my head.  The cushion on the top makes it even more comfortable.  I highly recommend this product whether for adults or for children.  Easy to use and absolutely worth more than the price listed for purchase.  Bought it!  Enjoying it!  Forever a customer at this point!

title: Cute, sounds ok, comfort for limited time
text:
I just want to say this headset is A.O.K. for the price. But you must understand that these are not exceptional headphones. They're budget head phones.

I'm trying to be objective here while keeping in mind this is a $35 headset. The sound is worth $35, as is comfort. But again, these are cheap. They are plastic. The quality of audio is good for the price benchmark.

If you're on a budget, then these will fit the bill. Just be aware, you get what you pay for. For me, these are just a pair of dedicated headphones I plug into my Audio Interface so I can play guitar/bass to record, or when I want to play without an amp (i.e. late at night).

I don't agree with other reviews saying they're just as good as Bose, Senns, etc. Just not try. But they are cheaper!

title: Right speaker stopped working after 1 month and i used ...
text: Right speaker stopped working after 1 month and i used them maybe 5 times and i dont play anything loud

title: Comfortable, portable, and sounds great.
text: I've been using these on my daily train commute.  They sound fantastic, are very comfortable, have a long battery life, and easy to use buttons.  The hinges on the ear cups are great for fit and portability.  This is my fourth set of commute headphones over the years, and I think these are the best yet.

title: So far Great. Would say yes to anyone in Music DJ ...
text: Works really well for my work as a DJ.  So far Great.  Would say yes to anyone in Music DJ clubs etc...

title: Great sound but do not last.
text: Bought my first pair 7/19...the swivel over the ear piece broke, got another pair on 11/19...they just broke in the same place....5/2020....so they only last between 4-6 months.  I wear them daily but do not fold them daily.  I am torn, I love them but am considering trying a different brand, even if they cost more because it would be worth it in the long run.

title: Great
text: Sounds great. Good fit. Going to use them on a long flight. Sure they will be great. Quality built for the price

title: Cheap Material. Poorly put together
text:
This is the second pair I’ve bought and no more Than 2months of moderate use and they are broken the same way AGAIN!!!
. I even purchased a hard case to protect them from transport damage. And the headphones snap by the ear swivel. Cheap manufacturing. You’ll save more money buying headphones with better quality that will last. Sound is good but the quality of the product is bad. And not worth the money.

UPDATE:
OneOdio has sent me a free replacement pair! The replacement pair are working correctly and sound great. Thanks.

title: Best by far!
text: Only had these for 3 days so this may be a premature review. So far the sound is WAY better than my Sony and Beats. Battery still says 100% and I have been rockin out since I charged them. Wearing them with no music helps to block out the yelling of a 4 yo boy. I haven't reached for any other earbuds or headphones. Sound quality on calls is great also, the caller can hear me just fine unlike some other brands. Would highly recommend!

title: Fit cant be customized
text: I have no idea whether these headphones do what they're supposed to do or not, as I couldn't get past the fit. The headband has no flexibility at all, and it was too wide and flat to fit my head. I didn't use them even once to listen to anything the with.

title: A total joke.
text: This I a total joke. The 1st one worked for about two weeks. I got a guaranteed replacement that broke also two weeks later.

title: Good sound but started breaking around a year
text: I got these headphones last year and they'worked well. The audio quality is good. I mainly use them to listen to music while doing homework, so they get a lot of daily use, especially since the start of the pandemic. A month or two the 3.5mm to 6.5mm cable stopped working so I started using a 3.5mm to 3.5mm cable I had. Now withing the past week the swivel on the side with the 6.5mm jack broke so now it will randomly dislodge and flap around by my ear. The slightest movement can dislodge it and it's been getting worst over the past few days. I can pop it back into place and the audio quality is fine but if I so much as tilt my head too far in one direction or move my hair from under the headphones, it will pop out. I'm disappointed that it broke like this because I did really like these headphones but they just haven't lasted as long as I would have liked. Up till a week ago, I had no complaints other than the mild annoyance of the cable stopping working. They are comfortable, have goo

title: Look elsewhere if you have a massive noggin
text: Too tight for my big old head. If you have a non melon sized head, give it a look!  Sound quality is great.

title: Meh
text: Very cheap plastic. All materials that make these headphones are low quality. Sound is ok. If you plan on handling these with kid gloves then they are ok. Not worth more than $20-$30 though.

title: Others can hear 30 feet away
text:
My son said these had terrible noise cancellation and he can not use them. They are basically like walking around with speakers on your head! He tried to use them at the library and people 30 feet away could hear what he was listening to! He often uses speakers to make calls on his phone too and that was not even possible.
They defeat the purpose of being headphones if they are simply speakers for all to hear.
He had to use cheap earbuds the library loaned him.
He said these grey and tan ones looked a bit girlish. I as a mom thought they were attractive. I think the tan may look a bit like light pink possibly and he said girls gave him compliments lol which may mean they are more to a girls liking. I got this color due to he is on the street a lot and can not be drawing attention to himself. He can not have red or blue gang colors which some of the others had even if it was in the cord. And the black ones had shiny chrome which is too much of an attention getter. So, I o

title: Owned for 2 years and...
text:
I have now owned these for over 2 years. I use them daily listening to music on my laptop, and as of 6/2/2022, I have them plugged into my Focusrite Scarlett 3rd Gen 2i2. You are seeing over 15k reviews for a reason.
The cost and the quality.
Now, I do not have a comparison to any other headphones but the quality is great even after testing it on the 'ultimate headphone test' on youtube.

Pro's
Cost
Has true surround
Function & Form

Con's
Lack of color variety
Folds up weird (for my taste)
The hair on the top of my head sticks straight up after wearing them for a while

Just get them, the price is perfect for what you get.

title: the audio is decent but their is a couple problems.
text:
TL:DR
Materials: Cheap.

Sound quality: quite Decent but a bit heavy on the lower ends and weak on the higher ends.

Over Ears: For people with larger than average heads or slightly larger ears than a 10 year old you will notice the headphones are more like on ear instead of being over ear.

the quality of materials feel a bit on the cheep side of materials, The only part of this headset that is metal are the adjustment strips and the arch band on the inside of the padding so they would wear out slower, all the other parts are plastic and i could see many of them breaking quickly if the user is someone who takes their headset off many times a day and plops them on their desk.

The Speakers/Drivers are decent for audio and the noise cancelling works as you may expect .

My problem with these headphones is if you have a slightly larger head than average you may struggle to have them sit over your ears correctly and they will tend to

title: Innovative design features; hopeless audio
text: The good: detachable cables and the ability to connect to different size jacks is a wonderful feature that I wish all of my headphones would incorporate. The bad: I don't know why these are marketed as studio reference headphones. The bass is ridiculously loud and the mids are weak.

title: Good for the price, but makes you look like an alien...
text:
I purchased these as a replacement for an older pair of Monster NTunes that broke. Overall, the sound is good. They seem flimsy, the ear coverings are huge (pics don't accurately depict how huge they look on). The swivel parts seem like they could break easily if you weren't extra careful. I would up giving these headphones to my 9-year-old who would only wear them inside the house while on her tablet. I think they'd look silly if you were walking down the street with them or using them at the gym.

For the price, a nice pair ... for indoors.

title: wonderful
text: Very comfortable. I don't say that lightly. I am persnickety about fit and comfort. I use these to copy morse code for long periods . These are very comfortable.  Sound quality is very good. Being hard of hearing I hear a good morse code tone. I am sure they will be very good for music

title: NO VOLUME CONTROL
text: I purchased this item after ready many of the reviews. I guess I should have asked questions. I connected them to my TV when they arrived. I didn't realize that the had no volume control. Some programming is louder than others and the volume needs to be lowered. Some programming volume is lower and needs to be turned up. If you want to control the volume, these are not the ones to choose.

title: You can't lose with these headphones!
text: I absolutely love these headphones! They are so affordable and the quality of this product is excellent ffçç in in. And on top of that, they sound fantastic. What more could you ask for? Highly recommended!

title: There's better out there
text: Volume and sound are weak, even for the price. The cord is irritating to say the least. Buyer beware. Or Don't. You will not jam to your fave music with these.

title: Weird sound quality, like you are in the bottom of a well
text: Having to send back. Reviews sounded great. Ours aren't so great. You can hardly hear audio, sounds like you are really far away, at the bottom of a well.

title: Not a good buy.
text: Plastic, made cheap, headphones are constantly rotating the other way.

title: Good sound, awful durability
text: Had these for four months before both cans snapped off at the joint where they rotate. Sound quality is good, but not worth the cost.

title: Surprise
text: I didn’t expect how good it is. Hubby want to buy it for our keyboard but I thought any headphone can do. It was big difference! Much better quality than the other headphones around home.

title: Nice for the price
text: We bought them for school.  They’re cheap but work as expected.

title: Too much bass; won't extend enough for a big head
text: Looks and feels substantial.  Detachable cords with choice of jack sizes is a nice feature, though coiled cord with the 6.35 mm jack doesn't stretch as easily as it should.  Sound volume and quality is, for the most part, very good, but there's somewhat too much bass.  I listen to classical music, while it appears that most reviewers who gave 5 stars listen to rock or rap.  The other deficiency is that I can't extend the headband far enough to go over the top of my head.  I have to wear the headband either forward or behind the top of my head.

title: Too many swivels in the set
text: The head phones flip flop too much when you try to use them

title: LOVE!
text: Great product for the price they are comfortable and exactly what I needed. I would recommend.

title: Work for me
text: Over the ear, non-powered.  Sound quality is good and the unit is comfortable.  Amazon's rating system asks about noise cancellation, but these are not NC headphones.  They work well for the price

title: I was impressed
text: I use these for over the ear monitors in a band. I like the versitility of the multiple positions it will form to. They seem pretty sturdy because the catch a lot of abuse on stage. They can handle the Bass being driven thru them in the monitor mix.

title: Barely acceptable for any use
text:
The high ratings on the OneOdio Wired Over Ear Headphones Studio Monitor & Mixing DJ Stereo Headsets
doesn't make sense to me. The frequency response, dynamic range, and sound clarity are poor. There is a good reason why decent headphones cost over $100 The claim in some reviews that these are good for use in a studio or for DJ mystifies me.

title: WONDERFUL HEADPHONES
text: Wonderful headphones I love mine and it's hard to beat this price. But be aware there is a small issue the 9ft. length cord is only possible if you stretch them out and when you do the only way to keep them from coming off is to hold them on by hand. So I purchased an audio cord to go with them, ( PlugLug 3.5 mm 12ft. Premium Auxiliary Audio Cable ) was $9.99 there a great buy.

title: These could deafen you!
text: I can't use these because they are too loud and there is no way to adjust the volume.  If the seller has something with volume control I'd like to trade these in!

title: Lasted a while, not as long as I would have liked
text:
Bought these headphones in January and worked all the way up until about 2 weeks ago. These were hooked up to a blue yeti microphone and when I went to adjust something on my desk and went to put my hand back, i caught the cord and it messed up the sound. I was no longer getting audio out of one side of the headset and had to half plug in the jack to keep the buzzing noise to a minimum. Granted this was mostly at my fault but still, I would expect headphones to have higher quality to tough through plenty.

Not all was negative about these though. The sound quality was great for the time they worked. When playing games, I was able to hear my surroundings enough to not be a nuisance. The bass was there but not overwhelming (not what i bought them for) and they were very comfy to wear for 3+ hours at a time.

In short, these are great headphones for the price but were not as durable as I would have liked them to be, if you buy a pair, be sure to be careful around

title: decent sound quality, not the best build quality
text:
Sound quality is decent. They just broke, I guess I set them down a little too hard? I don't even recall being that rough. I had these for just a year. I've had other headphones for 10 years. I also don't really like the way they fold up and spin, I guess it's convenient for travel but it means i have to un-spin and readjust them almost every time I pick them back up.

Update: they gave me a refund, I do admire their warranty. For this reason, there is less risk to give it a try if you're on the fence.

title: Good quality for the money
text: They are good overall, but Im not sure if they can be used as studio monitor headphones, i think they have too much bass.

title: Poor sound quality and no bass response
text: These have no richness of sound or bass response. I really do not understand how they got so many good reviews. To me they are not worth the money. I would not recommend.

title: Great headphones. Glad to own them.
text: This works well with my Behringer U-Phoria box and the GarageBand software on my MacBook Pro. It is a fine addition to meet my music monitoring needs.

title: Great quality for the price
text: Im never gonna stop using these head phones. They work great plugged into my bass amp. They're awesome on my PC. And I wear plugs so they fit comfortable over my ears even with tapers. Highly recommended

title: Feel Comfortable to Wear
text: These fit good and are not hard to wear. They were just what I was looking for so that I could play my piano without disturbing my wife while she is reading. I should have got these a long time ago because I would practice more often. My wife says that I don't pick the best times to play, she might be right, but I do it when I think of it. Now I can do it anytime. They work good and I don't have any issues with them. Buy them you will like it.

title: Material is cheapooooo!!!!
text: Material is cheap , sound is good . The head piece is junk. Will be uncomfortable to wear longer than 15 min. Would not buy again .

title: Girly headphones for girly girls who can't handle MANLY sound
text:
I never knew headphones could have female genitalia! I wish I'd discovered these sooner. My prior head experiences have all been sorely lacking. Who knew that I just needed to swing to the other side of the fence to get more sound satisfaction? These do chafe my girly skin a bit after extended use, but hey, being sore the morning after is all part of the fun, right?

Seriously, I knocked two stars off the rating for the pointless gendered crap. Just call it pink or rose gold and let the buyer decide if they want the color, without bringing stupid gender crap into it. I can see the color. Any buyer can see the color. We don't need the stupid "FOR WOMEN" disclaimer. The only good thing about that nonsense is that this color is cheaper than the so-called MANLY alternatives. The sound quality is good -- I use these as my gaming headphones, as regular headsets hurt my scalp and ears -- the mic quality is clear (according to my guildmates over Discord), so, yeah, these are a good,

title: Great headphones
text: I use this for my electric drum set. This has two plugs and two different points of access for the aux cords....which are included. I can play with my Spotify and hear both the drum set and the song great!

title: good until they break, which may not take long.  after two pairs failed, i'm over these.
text:
Definitely not recommended. I've had two pairs. This time it was my left headphone speaker that died with no warning. Last time, it was the right. I'm careful how I handle them, but...oh well.

The headphones themselves stopped fitting about three months ago. The nifty (plastic) assemblies that let the headphones rotate aren't very sound. I don't rotate mine, but the shafts on both sides lost their anchors in their sockets a month ago. Had to wear a cap to keep them from slipping off my head ever since.

Note that the manufacturer's extended warranty is only available when the headphones are purchased directly from their website. Purchases made on Amazon not supported.

FIRST REVIEW: These are comfortable, and the sound quality's very good. The flippable DJ-friendly headphones take some getting used to.

But my right headphone died today without warning or discernable cause. The headphones were working just fine thismorning...plugged t

title: One Of The Best Budget Headphones
text: I was blown away with the quality of these headphones. I was in need of some versatile headphones I could use when recording my parts on the eight string baritone guitar. Because of the range on these guitars, it is hard to find something that will pick up the higher notes while not muddying the lower notes. These headphones are amazing as each note on the guitar is extremely clear, making them a must have. What I also find a unique bonus is the interchangeable 1/4 and 1/8 jack inputs. These become extremely handy to listen to both regular music and my guitar. For fun, I tried listening to some really deep bass tones, and these headphones held up wonderfully. If you want some great headphones without breaking your bank, these are the way to go.

title: Seems like good quality
text: I haven’t used them much yet, but the quality seems good - and the sound is great.

title: This headphone set is an excellent product, better than the Bose they are replacing.
text: I bought this in PINK for my wife, and was impressed enough with it that I bought a second set in BLACK for myself. These are substantial and well-made headphones; nothing about them feels or looks inexpensive. The headband is well padded and the adjustment fittings and attachment points appear to be very robust. I especially like the dual cords and headphone jacks, one 6.35mm plug on the left and one 3.5mm on the right; this allows me to keep the longer cable connected to my desktop, while keeping the shorter cable attached to my laptop. I can use the headphones on either machine just by switching cables at the headphone end. Very convenient! The frequency response is also excellent, and the large 50mm drivers probably help account account for this. Registering online gives an additional warranty period, extending to 24 months. Nice. The fit is comfortable for both my wife and me. I use the headband at full extension (I am male, 205 lbs, hat size 7 & 1/8) and my wife (female,125 lbs)

title: Not durable! Only lasted 6 months.
text: I thought I had really found some great headphones for a great price. They only lasted six months and a few weeks before the wire stopped making a connection. No way to contact the seller. Just out of luck!

title: They look impressive
text: Just some slight pressure on the glasses if you’re wearing them too long, otherwise they’re great...

title: These are the headphones you want !!!
text: Very comfortable and great sound quality ! Fits my phone jack perfectly ! I got a pink one for my wife and she loves it too !

title: Good Quality but Great Customer Service
text:
The headphones were pretty good given the price. I was looking for a standard, comfortable quality cheaper set for airplane rides or relaxing at home and saw this product. It doesn't feel flimsy or cheap quality, but for some reason, mine stopped working in less than a month. I do know I dropped it from my bed to the floor, but that shouldn't have caused the headphones to break entirely (no physical damage as it was maybe a 2 foot drop).

Going to look out for another pair of headphones, maybe a better known brand and hopefully will not have to re-order after that.

Edit: Rachel from oneodio reached out (without me having to even contact them myself) and worked with me to get a replacement sent over. Received it and it's working great like before!

title: The bass hits HARD on these.
text: For the price, A+. No brainer purchase.

title: Not good for DJing... they're actually annoying...
text: I'm not impressed.  These things are very difficult to use as they keep flipping all over the place.  They are not comfortable.  I would not buy again.  I'm disappointed...  after reading the reviews I thought they would be good... NOT good for DJing.

title: Good Sound, Comfortable, Fantastic Buy
text: As other reviewers have noted, they're a tiny bit flat in the mids, but that can easily be tweaked with a little EQ adjustment. The materials seem robust, and there's no 'feedback' from rubbing the cord. There is a noticeable difference in sound quality on the two cords; the longer, coiled cord is good. The other, not so much. I really like the look of them, they look like they cost two or threes times as much. I have pretty large flappers, but the ear cups are generous and I can, with periodic adjustment, wear them comfortably for several hours. The only nit, and it is just that, is the tiny grooves cut on the sides are dust collectors as expected from the photos. Bought these for the office. May pick up another pair for the house as well. Overall, nice cans!

title: Able to isolate sounds
text: I got these for my son to use with his iPad with his auditory processing therapy.  He loves them and they work perfectly for his needs.

title: Very nice sound and comfort.
text: This headset with two wire choices, one with a wire mic, is a comfy fit for an average ear or smaller, and emits an excellent sound quality. I used it to record and sing on Stramaker and I could hear the music much clearer , enabling more fine tuning in my vocal recording. So far, so good! Hope they are durable and last.

title: One Odin Headphones
text: They are comfortable and they sound great.  They block quite a bit of outer sound .

title: THIS Is How Music Is Supposed To Sound
text: Multiple ways to connect to various devices, can be piggy-backed several together to share the music. Very comfortable for long wear. Recommend!

title: Good but Cheap
text: While these headphones really do deliver very good sound with their 50mm drivers and good seal between the ears and padding, the only parts I could find that weren't plastic were the screws. Boy are these cheap feeling. But in the end they're comfortable and sound great.

title: Voice is not stereo.
text: It’s not that good, what I thought. Practically my previous earphone is better than this one.

title: Not worth the low price
text: These looked very nice and inexpensive, have 2 jacks to choose from but they simply stopped working after 4 months of use. Cheap, yes, but not worth the price. I do not recommend this brand to anyone. Highly rated but a waste of money.

title: It's ok
text: Not that impressed with these headphones. There is NO volume control on them and the noise cancellation is almost nonexistent, it's almost like wearing ear buds.

title: Great Gift
text: Now my daughter wears them all the time, so she can listen to what ever she wants too without bothering anyone else.

title: No connectors in the box
text: I must have been sent a return. No cable in the box, no instructions, no nothing. What a joke

title: Seem to be good quality headset.
text: These don't actually completely cover the ears, but they do hold out outside noise fairly well. we'll see how they hold up.

title: No one else wants to hear your tunes and you'll be in heaven!
text: Be aware these have zero volume adjustment, whatever comes from your sound source is what you get, dial it there. DJs aren't into volume anyway, right (?) Also, the ear cushions are definitely not too big (if you have a small head /ears you'll be fine, I have a large head and gauges lol) and do a good job of isolating the sound to what you're listening to; comfortable and not hot. An audiophile friend rates the sound quality as incredible. To me, for 35 bucks, they are an awesome alternative to speakers. I got a second pair because they chain together easily with included cables (everyone listens at the same volume, right!) Yeah, man!

title: Great for traveling
text: Used on my last flight and they worked just as expected.

title: Great headphones
text: First, I was skeptical about the price. To good to be true prices. These headphones are great, the sound with bright colors is exquisite. Easy on the ears.

title: Very happy with my purchase!
text: Quality headphones, which comes with 2 adapter wires. One has 6.35mm and 3.5 mm plugs., and the other has 3.5 mm at both ends. Quality sound and great price! $32

title: Cheap but you can tell. Decent though.
text: Not great sound but a good deal. I wish the ear cushions were a hit softer. I wear glasses and I can feel the headphones pushing the earstalks into my skull.

title: Low quality. Buy instead Anker headphones.
text: Low quality. Buy instead Anker headphones.

title: Seem to be good quality for the price and work with my cell phone.
text: Seem to be good quality for the price and work with my cell phone. However, there is not way to control the volume that I can find.  Overall happy with this purchase and hope they hold up as others I have purchased only last a few months.

title: Husband happy!
text: Husband enjoying his earphones!

title: Good sound!
text: The audio is very good but the cable wore out the first week. Ordered a replacement and it works fine.

title: My son is very happy
text:
The sound is excellent
Plays electric drum set

title: These are  Outstanding
text: Got the sound back after a year of not having any on my PC. I had to buy a new DAC FX 6 which I bought brand new on eBay only paid 57.00 and change. I got these headphones and let me tell you guys they are outstanding I compared with my SennIhiser and believe I got not tell the difference after watching my music videos. High Quality at a very affordable price. Must buy these phones they are very comfortable to wear. The sound quality is just superb. Best buy ever will be getting this brand from here on out.

title: Excellent battery life
text: Works well on conference calls  (speaking and listening).  I have been on nine hour calls without recharging.  Great sound quality of the price!

title: Great product at this price point.
text: Would buy again, excellent battery life and good sound.

title: Loving it
text: I have had all headphones from expensive to cheap...those are perfect!

title: Very good sound quality
text: I really loved the sound quality.

title: Are as good as they look.
text: Comfortable, great looking, excellent sound quality and love the long cable from headphones to player.

title: Used with digital piano.
text: The product was delivered on time, packaging was in good condition, the product was in good condition and appeared to be as described, and after use met expectations for fit, form, and function.

title: Just what our son wanted
text: Son using to keep music and Zoom meetings from disturbing the rest of his family.

title: For the money the best I tried, and I tried...many.....
text: For the price these are the best headphones I found. they are for work and were the closest match to the sound of my Sony 7506's it's uncanny. They are a little to bassy, and flat but for the price they are awesome to keep at the office.
